conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now let me help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To get started, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children under the age of 16, or children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. Alex is 20 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Just to confirm - you mentioned Alex is 20 years old. Is Alex currently in any approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me confirm one more detail to give you a complete assessment.

Does Alex work, or receive any benefits in their own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work and doesn't receive any benefits in their own right.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the official UK government rules, I need to inform you of the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

Unfortunately, you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**The reason:** Child Benefit is only available for:
- Children under 16, OR
- Children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training

Since Alex is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, they fall outside the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.

If Alex's circumstances change in the future (though at age 20, even returning to education wouldn't qualify), or if you have any other questions, please feel free to ask.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, they do not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or those aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and not in approved education or training. As Alex exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit (which is under 20 and in approved education/training for those 16+), the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, they do not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or those aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and not in approved education or training. As Alex exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit (which is under 20 and in approved education/training for those 16+), the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
